title: In Memoriam: Alan Greenspan | Council on Foreign Relations
gist: Alan Greenspan, who served five terms as Federal Reserve Chairman from 1987 to 2006 under four presidents, has died; his tenure included the post-Cold War period, the Asian Financial Crisis, and the lead-up to the 2008 financial crisis, earning him the reputation as 'maestro' of the US economy.

insights.claim: Alan Greenspan served five terms as Federal Reserve Chairman from 1987 to 2006 under four presidents, navigating the post-Cold War period, the Asian Financial Crisis, and the lead-up to the 2008 financial crisis—earning the reputation as 'maestro' of the US economy.
insights.speaker: Council on Foreign Relations
insights.so_what: Greenspan's death removes one of the last first-hand architects of the modern global financial system; his era of 'Greenspan put' monetary policy—suppressing volatility through accommodation—shaped investor expectations that persist today.
insights.quote: He led the central bank from 1987 to 2006 through periods of both prosperity and crisis, including the post-Cold War period, the Asian Financial crisis, and the lead-up to the 2008 financial crisis.

insights.claim: Greenspan was a CFR member since 1978 and board director from 1982 to 1988—a cross-institutional link between the Federal Reserve and the foreign policy establishment that characterized the coordination between US monetary policy and geopolitical strategy during his era.
insights.speaker: Council on Foreign Relations
insights.so_what: Greenspan's passing marks the end of an era when central bank leadership overlapped with foreign policy networks; the institutional distance between the Fed and geopolitical bodies today is significantly wider.
